page: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It also includes 1890 GB of local SSD storage and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most standard workloads, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2238.28 and a multi-core score of 34203.68. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which assist in specialized workloads. Single-threaded gzip compression performs in the top tier at 36.05 GB/sec, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong at 180.33 GB/sec. Large-block memory bandwidth reaches 146.04 GB/sec for read operations and 62.52 GB/sec for write operations.

For machine learning tasks, the hardware achieves a strong prompt processing speed of 190.8 tokens/sec on medium 7B LLM models, but text generation on large 70B models is a notable bottleneck, yielding a weak 2.69 tokens/sec. Given its high memory density and local storage capacity, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound applications that do not require GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data processing pipelines, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv7 family, powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 256.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per vCPU) and 1890 GB of local storage with Premium Storage support. The CPU features a large 480 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities alongside strong multi-threaded decompression and top-tier single-threaded gzip compression. However, text generation for large 70B LLM models is a performance bottleneck. This configuration is highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, including database hosting, Redis caching, and medium-scale data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, designed for database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-capacity local storage, and advanced matrix extension support for enterprise databases.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
256.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio
1890 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector operations
Optimized for relational databases, Redis caching, and data processing
